steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The function is $z = (0.829)^{2w}$. We need the percent decrease in $z$ for each 1-unit increase in $w$. That means we need the ratio $z(w+1)/z(w)$, then convert to a percent change.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '17.1%': would mean each step multiplies by $0.829$ (a 17.1% drop), but the exponent is $2w$, not $w$, so the multiplier per step is $(0.829)^2$, not $0.829$. ✗
  • Choice B — '29.3%': not a standard rounding of $(0.829)^2 - 1$. ✗
  • Choice C — '31.7%': off from the correct computation. ✗
  • Choice D — '31.3%': $(0.829)^2 = 0.687241$, so $z$ retains 68.72% each step — a decrease of $1 - 0.6872 = 0.3128 \approx 31.3\%$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
